title: Cinderella

evidence.note: Published 1914; the 95-year US copyright term has expired for works published through 1930, so the film print is public domain in the US by term expiry.

diy_links.label: Catalog of Copyright Entries (archive.org) — browse the Motion Pictures renewal-year volume by hand
US Copyright Office public records (1978+ renewals/assignments)

editorial: Cinderella is a 1914 American silent fantasy film directed by James Kirkwood Sr. and released by the Famous Players Film Company, starring Mary Pickford in the title role opposite her then-husband Owen Moore as the Prince. An early feature adaptation of the Perrault fairy tale, it is notable for its trick photography and double-exposure effects. As a 1914 US publication, the film print is public domain by expiry of the 95-year US copyright term; other layers remain undetermined.
